node.text:
For a 12th consecutive year, J.P. Morgan Private Bank is thrilled to partner with the internationally renowned art fair Paris Photo. 
 
We are excited to showcase a collection of images, which represent Global Visions and Local Voices. These themes are at the very core of our DNA, and express our local commitment and impact as a global institution.

node.text: Outstanding individuals. Exceptional opportunities. Watch as Carly Pierpoint, Head of Global Private Bank Solutions Talent Acquisition and Growth Strategy, discusses the people, resources and empowering culture that make careers with us professionally and personally rewarding. Explore more and find your future with the Private Bank.

node.text: Follow us in Chicago at our Emerging Family Leaders event. Watch our Windy City adventures, meet some inspiring speakers and enjoy the Private Bank experience. You haven’t seen Chicago until you’ve seen it through our eyes.

U.S. Treasury yields have taken a starring role in the latest episode of market volatility—behind the scenes we’re seeing opportunity. Read this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways for a closer look at the moves in rates and how investors can respond.
 
Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Under pressure? The three-and-a-half-year moratorium on student debt payments just ended, affecting roughly 40 million Americans who financed their educations with loans. Most of those borrowers are in the Millennial and Gen Z camps.
 
But while the clock is back on, borrowers can make use of a one-year grace period and skip payments without penalty (though interest will still accrue). To that end, many borrowers have signaled they plan to take up the offer and delay making payments, at least for a few months.
 
So while repaying loans could pinch some pocketbooks, the impact won’t be felt all at once—and we don’t think it’s enough to break the backs of broader consumers. Most who want a job still have one, and economy-wide household finances look well equipped to handle the challenges ahead. Overall, when adjusting U.S. household debt payments as a percentage of disposable income, things still look okay.

There’s no shortage of risks in today’s markets: Government shutdown fears, surging bond yields, lingering inflation and more. But there’s also plenty of reason for optimism. #TopMarketTakeaways takes a close look at investors’ pain points and makes the case for stronger markets ahead.
 
Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Shutdown showdown. Congress has to pass a short-term spending deal (a “continuing resolution”) by midnight on September 30 to keep the government’s lights on. Without it, all “nonessential” departments, such as the Environmental Protection Agency, the Labor Department and parts of the IRS, could be forced to power down. In essence, the government “shuts down.” 
 
We’ve seen a handful of government shutdowns before, with the longest in 2018 (which lasted over a month). But while the impact grows the longer it lasts, the hit to growth and stocks typically fades quickly once the government reopens. Looking at the last 21 government shutdowns (from when they started to when they ended), U.S. stocks have actually been about flat on average.

There could be volatility ahead as policymakers work to hammer out a deal, but in the medium to longer term, we think the primary drivers for markets will likely remain the same: the Federal Reserve, interest rates, earnings and incoming economic data.

For Climate Week, Jessica Matthews, Global Head of Sustainable Investing and William Sinclair, Head of U.S. Family Office for J.P. Morgan Private Bank hosted a candid discussion on how private markets can help solve water scarcity and achieve sustainability in the U.S. and Internationally, and are pictured here with two of the featured speakers Matt Damon, Actor, Co-founder of Water.org & WaterEquity and Christopher Allwin, CEO of Aetos Capital.  Other speakers included Water.org’s Vedika Bhandarkar and Gary White, Christopher Teryazos from Aetos Capital, and Clay Landry from West Water.

Amid today’s economic uncertainty, many investors are sitting in the safety of cash. With a yield of over 5%, it may seem pretty competitive. But is it really your best bet? In today’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we explore why we think other assets may offer more compelling opportunities in the months to come.
 
Tap the link in bio to learn more.

And just like that, the Federal Reserve hit “pause” after 525 basis points worth of rate hikes over the last year-and-a-half.
 
More in focus, though, is what the Fed signaled for the months and years ahead. With growth holding up but inflation still sticky, policymakers implied they plan to keep rates “higher for longer”—with potentially one more hike later this year and less cuts in 2024 and 2025 than initially projected.
 
Where we stand: Whether the Fed is done hiking or has one more move left, the era of rate hikes is coming to an end. Policymakers have still made a lot of progress, quelling price pressures with minimal economic pain. Of course, there are still risks (the list is long—from oil prices to strikes, to student loan payments, to a government shutdown), but in all, the data continues to point to a greater chance of a soft landing.
 
There may be swings along the way, but we still see the potential for markets to power through over the next year.

The price of oil is at its highest of the year, topping $90 per barrel for the first time since last November. But while there are risks, we think prices at the pump will likely ease in the months to come. #TopMarketTakeaways
 
Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Things heated back up in the latest U.S. inflation report. Headline CPI—which looks at a basket of consumer goods and services—accelerated to a 3.7% year-over-year pace in August from 3.2% in July. Higher gas prices this summer were mostly to blame.
 
But is it something to worry about?
 
The drivers of inflation are undergoing an evolution—but that might be okay. For one, inflation has come a long way from the sweltering 9% pace last summer. But more importantly, we don’t see a sustained rise in oil prices (barring an unexpected geopolitical shock), and rent prices and wages are also still cooling.
 
Of course there are always risks. But this is not 2022. Inflation is not accelerating without abandon, and central banks have hiked a lot to get on top of the problem. To us, this means the outlook still looks brighter today—and we see the potential for markets to power through.
 
CPI refers to Consumer Price Index from the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

Aside from this week’s volatility, the so-called “Magnificent 7” have been on a meteoric rise this year. Together, Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia and Tesla have accounted for over 70% of the S&P 500’s year-to-date return. They’ve also added as much in market cap as the size of Japan’s economy!
 
In a year when AI has been all the rage, these companies have benefited from the promise of new technology, but 2023’s gains have also come after a rough 2022 that saw the tech sector refocus and retrench.
 
Where do we go from here? Do this week’s swings signal new pressure points? At the least, it’s worth noting that the other 493 companies in the S&P 500 haven’t gotten the same love. With better prospects for U.S. growth and team “soft landing” gaining steam, the rally could continue and broaden out—and offer compelling value for investors.

We were proud to return as a sponsor for the 2023 Martha’s Vineyard African-American Film Festival #MVAAFF, a celebration that has recognized Black excellence in film for the last 21 years. During the festival, we partnered with Cartier to host an exclusive cocktail reception and dinner, as well as a fireside chat with Debra Lee, who discussed her book, “I Am Debra Lee,” and offered insights from her time as the CEO and Chairman of Black Entertainment Television (BET). 
 
Pictured here are Debra Lee, teams from Cartier and J.P. Morgan Private Bank, Leslie Wims Morris, President, Private Label Captive Finance, Chase Auto, as well as Stephanie Tavares-Rance, Co-Founder of the MVAAFF.

Thanks again to Tracy for joining Private Bankers Kimberly Rose and Maryann Zaleski in an engaging fireside chat, making our Women & Wealth luncheon unforgettable.  #jpmorganprivatebank #usopen

Earnings season is near its end, and at least for Corporate America, recession obsession seems to be fading. Rather, more firms are starting to focus on how they can continue growing in the long term. 
 
Mentions of “AI” have skyrocketed, with companies across industries ramping up investments. Nvidia’s blowout earnings report last week was a case in point. The chipmaker signaled that already strong demand could get even stronger, boosting its sales expectations for this coming quarter to ~$16 billion—more than 20% above Street forecasts. 
 
These kinds of numbers mean the benefit stretches beyond just Nvidia, and the real judge will be which companies successfully integrate AI and use it effectively over the next few years.

node.text: Aiming higher, boldly looking forward. It’s how champion players rise to the top. As the 2023 US Open begins, we’re proud to be sponsors for the 42nd year. We cheer on these many talented athletes, whose games are always exciting, always on. #usopen #jpmorganprivatebank

Staying invested never goes out of style.

Diversification and time are some of an investor’s most powerful allies when it comes to growing wealth over the long run. 
 
Markets can always have a bad day, week, month, or even year. But history suggests investors are less likely to suffer losses over longer periods, especially when utilizing diversification. In fact, an allocation balanced between U.S. stocks and bonds* hasn’t suffered a negative return over any five-year rolling period (or longer) in the past 70 years! Past performance doesn’t promise future results, but that’s a compelling track record. 
 
Every investor’s portfolio will look different depending on their unique set of financial goals, ability and willingness to take risk, and time horizon. Assets such as stocks and bonds are merely vehicles to get you to where you want to go on your financial journey. The roadmap is your financial plan.
 
*Portfolio allocation refers to 50% U.S. stocks and 50% U.S. bonds. U.S. stocks represent the S&P 500 Shiller Composite. U.S. bonds represent an average of Strategas/Ibbotson government and corporate bonds for periods from 1950 to 2017, then an average of Bloomberg’s U.S. Treasury Index and Bloomberg’s U.S. Aggregate Bond Index from 2017 to 2022.

What’s next for investors? To find out, #TopMarketTakeaways checks in with the Fed, Wall Street, Main Street and the C-suite for the latest reads on the markets and economy.

Pictured here are Private Bankers Nick Harris and Marco Narvaez during another great Aspen ArtWeek and ArtCrush Gala.

node.text:
Despite all the angst about high interest rates, the total value of homes in America hit a record high of $47 trillion in June.* And as millennials grow into prime home-buying age, they’re making some of the biggest gains.
 
Over the last year, the total value of homes owned by the millennial crew has increased almost 3% to $5 trillion—now officially outstripping that of the Silent Generation (those folks born before 1946). What’s more, millennials make up some 60% of buyers who have purchased homes with mortgages over the last few years.**
 
The prevalence of 30-year, fixed-rate mortgages may have something to do with it, with many buyers locking in low rates before they shot higher. At the same time, a decade-plus of underbuilding after the Global Financial Crisis has meant a lower supply of houses to go around, acting as a support for home prices.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

*Redfin. Published August 11, 2023.
**Federal Reserve. March 31, 2023.

This year’s big debates feel tired—all investors are seeming to hear about is inflation, monetary policy and government debt. But outside the normal headline swirl, there’s more to consider, such as budding IPOs, labor strikes and more. This week’s #TopMarketTakeaways takes a quick look at dynamics we think offer promise, and others that could have the potential to trip up investors.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

We recently gathered in Aspen, Colorado, to discuss a variety of wealth management topics in a relaxed and open setting. The Private Bank hosted a series of events that included a women’s wine tasting with Monica DiCenso, J.P. Morgan’s Head of Global Opportunities; a luncheon with Ben Hesse, Chief Financial Officer for Asset & Wealth Management, and Tom Kennedy, Chief Investment Strategist; and a cocktail party at a leading Aspen art gallery. Spending time with our clients in Aspen is something we look forward to every year.

The July U.S. inflation report passed the vibe check. Core CPI (which strips out volatile food and energy prices, and is the metric the Federal Reserve is most focused on) is tracking at a 4.7% year-over-year pace—its coolest since October 2021.

You might have even noticed that some parts of this year’s summer vacay feel a little better: Energy prices are lower than where they were a year ago, airfares are seeing big declines, and used car prices fell again. It also looks like there’s ample runway for the cool summer to continue, especially for core services such as rent prices (which accounted for the bulk of inflation last month). Leading indicators, from Zillow to ApartmentList, have been pointing to a slowdown for months, even if it hasn’t shown up in the official data yet. 

In all, this is a welcome print, but probably not a huge game changer. It looks like the Fed is still at or nearing the end of its tightening cycle. And to us, this means that the 60/40 is here to stay.

Luxury goods manufacturers are becoming more eco-focused. At a recent client reception held at the Breitling Madison Avenue Flagship Boutique in New York, Jessica Matthews, Global Head of Sustainable Investing at the Private Bank, and Camilo Lopez, Chief Financial Officer and Chief Operating Officer, Breitling USA, led attendees through the latest industry innovations and trends, including a demonstration of sustainable watchmaking. #jpmorganprivatebank 

Photo credit: Velocity Images

On the heels of JPMorgan Chase’s 2023 Annual U.S. Bus Tour, Jamie Dimon hosted a reception on Martha’s Vineyard to celebrate the second anniversary of the firm’s Black Wealth Initiatives. His comments throughout the evening focused on clients, colleagues and communities, the business environment and more.

There are long-term challenges from growing U.S. debt, but Fitch’s downgrade doesn’t change our outlook for stronger markets this year. #TopMarketTakeways examines those concerns and this week’s volatility.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Growing older can come with increased vulnerabilities. Adequately protecting yourself, your loved ones and your assets requires a bit of planning and coordination with those you trust. Start with these steps and tap the link in our bio to learn more.

Amid the great “Barbenheimer” debate, markets and the economy are weighing in. While some parts of the economy are giving Oppenheimer vibes, stocks are rallying like it’s a Barbie world. #TopMarketTakeaways

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

End of an era? After over a year of tightening, the Federal Reserve may be drawing a curtain on its rate hikes. Following a “skip” at its June meeting, the Fed recently hiked rates by 25 basis points to a target policy rate of 5.25–5.50%. Just as expected.
 
But if that’s the end, are we out of the woods and in the clear? We doubt policymakers are throwing high-fives just yet, but it’s clear the economy has been stronger than expected while inflation has been having a cool summer.
 
Whether the Fed gets a reputation for achieving a soft landing or spurring a mild recession, stocks have moved on from the debate. Following a very rough 2022 as the Fed got going, it probably wasn’t even in most investors’ wildest dreams to see the S&P 500 up roughly 20% so far this year and less than 5% off of last January’s record highs. It’s also probably not a huge coincidence that the market bottomed around the time the Fed delivered its final monster 75-basis-point hike, slowing its pace from there.

Looking into the second half of the year, markets seem less concerned about a recession than anticipated. Here are some scenarios on our radar that investors may not have seen coming.

Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

One year ago, the United States was in the middle of a “vibe-cession.” Inflation was surging. Gas prices were high. And consumers felt worse about the economy than they did during the COVID lockdowns. But this summer, we’re seeing a big positive vibe shift. So why is everyone feeling better all of a sudden? Find out in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.
 
Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Being an effective nonprofit board member takes dedication, a willingness to learn and a whole lot of hard work. To narrow your search and then truly make an impact, consider a few steps that can put you on the right path.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

If you’ve been sitting on the sidelines while stocks roared higher in the first half of the year, you might be wondering if you’ve missed your chance to get in on the rally—and whether it’s still worth jumping in. This week, #TopMarketTakeaways explores the latest indicators and explains why we think there may yet be room to run.
 
Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Oh how times have changed. This week’s U.S. CPI print showed that inflation angst is fading into the rear-view mirror. The headline rate dropped 100 basis points to 3.0% year-over-year, marking its slowest pace since March 2021 and 12 straight months of cooling. The core measure (ex-food & energy) also fell to 4.8%, unseen since October 2021.
 
Under the hood, forces against inflation broadened further: Used auto prices fell after reaccelerating in recent months. Airfares and hotel prices plummeted, even as folks went on summer holidays, alongside declines from meat, dairy, non-alcoholic beverages and fuel prices.
 
Shelter costs (think: rent) and core services ex-shelter (think: your doctor, barber, barista) were still sticking points, but all in all, this marks a stark contrast to where we stood just a year ago when headline inflation hit its highest at 9.1%, and the Federal Reserve was just getting going on its rate hikes. Sweet relief.

J.P. Morgan Private Bank teams from Salt Lake City and Paris recently hosted Utah’s Governor-led trade mission in Paris at our historic office on Place Vendôme. Jointly organized by World Trade Center Utah and the Governor’s Office of Economic Opportunity, the mission brought together nearly 100 business and government leaders, and aimed to connect Utah companies with foreign partners, facilitate connections with France’s thriving startups and investment funds, and generate business opportunities.

Signs of life for the housing market?
 
Housing was one of the first sectors to crack under the weight of higher rates, but signs now are pointing to stabilization, and maybe even an acceleration. From housing starts to building permits, to homebuilder confidence, the data seems to be improving. Add to that recent stability in mortgage rates (even if they’re high) and a still-strong (albeit slowing) consumer, and you get more homebuyer traffic and demand for homes.  
 
This could be a sign that the economy can handle higher rates, but it’s also worth noting that the last decade of underbuilding has left housing inventories low. Either way, markets are taking notice: Homebuilder stocks are some of the best performers this year.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

An inspiring theme within the J.P. Morgan Summer Reading List 2023 comes to life from Gloria Chang Yip and Debra Langford of the Private Bank’s Diverse Wealth Initiatives. Introducing two children’s books—“A Library” by Nikki Giovanni and “Amazing” by Maia Shibutani, Alex Shibutani and Dane Liu—they discuss how sharing diverse perspectives through books can enlighten young children and shape their futures.
 
Tap the link in our bio to access the J.P. Morgan Summer Reading List.

We’re delighted to welcome our Asset & Wealth Management Summer Analysts to J.P. Morgan. Over the next few months, they will be part of teams that serve the needs of sophisticated institutions and individuals around the world. We look forward to an insightful and productive summer together.

node.text:
Celebrating ties to the United Kingdom for nearly 200 years, we’re delighted to announce that J.P. Morgan Private Bank has opened doors to its first-ever office in Manchester.
 
Hear firsthand from Oliver Gregson, Region Head, U.K., Channel Islands & Ireland, Khayyam Jumani, Team Head & Senior Client Advisor, U.K. North, and colleagues on what this milestone means for us.
 
Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

The Private Bank’s Black Wealth Initiatives Group in Washington, DC recently hosted an arts, culture and philanthropy event. The gathering featured two fireside chats: the first with Reggie Van Lee, Chief Transformation Officer at The Carlyle Group; and the second with Alicia Wilson, Managing Director and Global Head of Philanthropy for the North American Region at JPMorgan Chase. The discussions shed light on Reggie’s journey as a collector, the way art can tie into philanthropy, and best practices when it comes to giving.

Summer sun, inflation cooldown. This week’s read on U.S. Consumer Price Inflation (CPI) came pretty much in line with estimates, with headline prices decelerating to a 4.0% year-over-year pace in May and core (ex-food and energy) prices cooling to 5.3%. While that’s still too hot for the Federal Reserve’s comfort (and its 2% mandate), headline inflation has more than halved from its hefty 9.1% pace last June. What’s more, almost 80% of the goods and services in consumers’ price basket were running at a dangerously fast clip north of 6% just eight months ago. Today, that’s down to almost 30%, while the percentage of components below a 3% pace continues to grow.

node.text:
Despite (or perhaps because of) the recession obsession, economists have been underestimating the labor market for over a year. The actual number of jobs gained (as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics) has exceeded the median economist estimate in every month back to April 2022. Last month’s report confirmed that the economy added almost 340k jobs in May. Economists expected only 195k. Bulls say that more job gains buy the Federal Reserve time to generate a “soft landing.” Bears say the Fed will eventually have to crack the labor market to get inflation under control.
 
Tap the link in bio to find out where we stand, and how your portfolio could benefit, in our #MidYearOutlook.

A recent Asian Leadership Forum and Office of Asian & Pacific Islander Affairs event featured Emmy-winning filmmakers of “38 at the Garden,” Frank Chi, Travon Free and Samir Hernandez. The trio shared how their Asian and Black roots united to depict the real story behind Jeremy Lin’s season with the New York Knicks. “Linsanity” wasn’t just a sports phenomenon but a clear example of how stereotypes can impede the success of others and how breaking them, as Lin did, can have a transformative impact.

Despite all of 2023’s angst, stocks are on the rise. But is it all just big tech? This week, #TopMarketTakeaways explores this year’s rally and explains why we think it can broaden, even if economic growth slows.
 
Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
“The only thing driving markets higher this year is tech!” While tech has in fact been a powerhouse, taking a look around the world provides a slightly different perspective. Out of the nearly 50  stock markets around the world that we track, 16 countries are outperforming the United States. The heroic market rally is far wider than many investors realize—and compelling opportunity may just lie outside of our own stomping grounds.
 
For more of our insights as well as regional preferences, tap the link in our bio to read last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

At an intimate luncheon hosted by the Private Bank in New Orleans, noted fashion designer Lela Rose shared business lessons she has learned throughout her successful career. Speaking to an audience of female entrepreneurs in the home of Managing Director Katie LeGardeur, she highlighted the importance of collaborating with colleagues and adapting to the ever-changing retail environment.

Asset & Wealth Management’s Chief Communications Officer and Head of Corporate Art and History Darin Oduyoye was recently honored by the Drama League for his outstanding contributions to the theater industry. The League recognized his leadership and commitment with its Gratitude Award, created as a tribute to the community’s heroes, alongside honorees Lear deBessonet, André De Shields and Lin-Manuel Miranda.

Strong principles, innovation, diversification and a commitment to investing in people and technology all help position JPMorgan Chase and the Private Bank toward continued success. That message resounded clearly during the firm’s annual #JPMInvestorDay, where analysts, investors and employees heard from senior leaders, including Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon, and Mary Callahan Erdoes, CEO, Asset & Wealth Management.
 
Tap the link in our bio to watch key highlights from this year’s event, showcasing remarkable insights and thought-provoking discussions.

The year-to-date rally pushed Apple’s market cap above the entire U.S. small cap index. Skeptics say this is indicative of a stock market rally that has only been driven by a few mega-cap companies, while optimists are starting to see some opportunity in lower valuations for smaller companies.

For more of our latest thinking, be sure to read last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

Past performance is no guarantee of future results. It is not possible to invest directly in an index.

For 35 years, the Private Bank has been proud to support the Robin Hood Foundation in its mission to fight poverty in New York City. As a sponsor of the Foundation’s annual benefit gala, we’re pleased to announce that the event raised more than $61 million to help New Yorkers in need.
 
Nelle Miller, Co-Head of New York, and William Sinclair, Head of FIG and U.S. Family Office, hosted guests last night and are pictured with Gayle King, who was one of the event hosts.

The engines roared and the sun shone as we proudly sponsored the California Mille, a weeklong drive across the Golden State for car enthusiasts. From historic routes to scenic byways, this event took drivers on a journey through some of California’s most stunning roads. If you’re looking to fuel your passions, tap the link in our bio to see how we can support you every mile of the way.

The good news: Inflation is continuing to cool. The bad news: Wrangling over the debt ceiling has markets on edge. This week, #TopMarketTakeaways breaks down these factors and more as investors try to make sense of today’s markets. 
 
Tap the link in our bio for more market insights.

node.text:
The latest CPI print is in! April’s reading showed that while inflation is continuing to cool, it is still far too high. But interestingly, high prices may not be the biggest concern for businesses anymore. Looking at a survey conducted by the Federal Reserve of New York on small businesses, quality of labor has surpassed inflation as the single most important problem for the first time since the Federal Reserve began hiking its policy rate last year. Good workers may be harder to find.
 
For more of our latest thinking, be sure to read our recent #TopMarketTakeaways.

Three electrifying days of roaring engines, thundering cheers, checkered flags, and heart-pounding racing at the #MiamiGP have come to a close. Committed to raising the standard in all we do, we salute these dedicated professionals in another arena who show us how they do the same.

Are you or someone you know getting ready to tie the knot? Before saying “I do,” here are the 5 W’s to help you navigate premarital planning.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

As a proud sponsor of the #MiamiGP, we're inspired by the resilience and confidence of talented drivers, powering through tough turns, red flags and sharp curves to blaze across the finish line. We appreciate the resilience, collaboration and teamwork it takes to boldly navigate whatever lies on the road ahead.

It’s been a strong start to the year, but challenges remain—from inflation to bank stress aftershocks, to the debt ceiling. Are markets oblivious to the risks, or keenly aware of them?
 
Tap the link in our bio for more investment insights.
We’re excited to be back as a founding partner of the #MiamiGP, and proud to be a part of the impressive economic impact it has on the local community.

We are proud to partner with the LeBron James Family Foundation (LJFF), an organization dedicated to empowering and supporting kids and families with resources and a promise.
 
J.P. Morgan has had a deep partnership with LJFF for over 15 years, including the sponsorship of its new community center, House Three Thirty, which opened on March 30 in Akron. This innovative space will offer financial programming, job training and community gathering opportunities for all. We’re excited to offer specialized financial resources and advice to the families in the community through this new dedicated space.
 
Our team was honored to be part of the recent LJFF gala—celebrating the grand opening of House Three Thirty. 
 
At J.P. Morgan, we believe sound financial health is the foundation for strong households and resilient communities.

We’re proud to partner with the Newport Harbor Yacht Club to support the 2023 Baldwin Cup Team Race and those who competed in the regatta. This was an exciting event showcasing the power of teamwork to achieve success. 
 
Tap the link in our bio to learn more about how you can partner with our team in Orange County to reach your goals.

Incredible cars. Priceless connections. An unforgettable weekend at Amelia Island! We were proud to sponsor the 28th Annual Concours d’Elegance. Tap the link in our bio to learn how to turn your passions into pursuits.

Market report card: From exceeding expectations in core bonds to the reversal of fortunes for technology stocks, how has our market outlook fared so far in 2023? Read this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways to find out. Tap the link in our bio for more insights.

node.text:
Taking a closer look at the first quarter: Tech drove the market, while banks bore the brunt of fragile sentiment. Within three short months, the focus for investors has swung from hot inflation data and a resilient economy to bank failures and a looming U.S. recession.
 
Markets have seen big swings as well. An index of regional banks suffered over -20% in losses last quarter, pulling large banks down a less dramatic, yet painful, -7%. But there were also bright spots. Tech pulled the broader stock market to an impressive +7% gain, and bonds served as prudent diversifiers.
 
While markets seem keen to move on from the bank shock, we likely haven’t yet seen all the impacts. For more of our insights and how to best position portfolios, be sure to check out last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways

The latest insights from our 13th Annual Energy Paper surround the challenges facing the renewable transition—including slow grid expansion, the scramble to reshore production and the economics behind storage and solar power. Tap the link in our bio to read the full report by Michael Cembalest, Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy for J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management. #EOTM

Princess Padmaja Kumari Parmar, Executive Director of HRH Hotels and Founder of Friends of Mewar, and Yaman Sharma, Banker, Upstate New York Team, discussed the importance of preserving culture and passing down generational wealth. The Princess shared insights from the Mewar family, the world’s longest-serving dynasty, on how they have maintained their cultural heritage, history and art since 734 AD.
 
Attendees had the opportunity to learn about Friends of Mewar’s positive impact on the Indian region of Udairpur, ensuring that the family’s legacy continues for generations to come.

We probably haven’t seen the last of the fallout from the recent bank shock, but where are the risks, and how should investors respond? This week, #TopMarketTakeaways sizes up the potential impact.
 
Tap the link in our bio for more insights.

node.text:
We are thrilled to announce that J.P. Morgan Private Bank has been named the “World’s Best Private Bank” by Euromoney for the second year in a row, as well as receiving several other prestigious awards.
 
We are honored to be recognized among our peers at firms worldwide, and we take pride in our commitment to delivering exceptional value to our clients.
 
Awards or rankings are not indicative of future success or results. Tap the link in our bio for our full list of awards.

#jpmorganprivatebank

Congratulations to La Suiza team! We proudly sponsored the international Polo Open tournament: Copa J. P. Morgan in Medellin, Colombia. Four teams participated in the tournament with players from Colombia, Venezuela and Argentina. After the final we enjoyed an award ceremony and a party with the players and other participants. Thank you very much to Club Campestre and all the teams!

In honor of #WomensHistoryMonth, we celebrate the story of Rebekah Gordon, a UX Designer and Strategist, and a strong advocate for diversity and equity in the workplace. As a Black woman, Rebekah overcame numerous challenges in her career, from being the only person of color and woman in the room to dealing with power struggles and microaggressions.
 
In a previous role as a certified court mediator, Rebekah had to navigate racial and gender dynamics when negotiating with a disrespectful attorney. While she was ultimately able to reach an agreement with the attorney by acknowledging his role and ability, the experience highlighted the need for and value of increased diversity and empathy in the workplace. Rebekah believes that keeping doors open for diverse voices will lead to reconciliation, advancement and innovation—and is proud to work for a firm like J.P. Morgan that is committed to making this happen.

Are the fears of a 2008 repeat justified? This week, #TopMarketTakeaways examines the current banking turmoil and explores what’s different today between now and the last crisis.
 
Tap the link in our bio for more investment insights.

From homeless to hopeful, Payal’s story is one of resilience, strength, courage and inspiration. After experiencing a divorce and losing her job and home during the pandemic, Payal found herself struggling to provide for her young daughter. Her journey took a hopeful turn when she joined J.P. Morgan Private Bank as a Product Marketing Strategy Lead. Not only did she gain a new career, she also found support, mentorship and opportunities to lead and grow, both professionally and personally. #WomensHistoryMonth

The Dubai Duty Free Tennis Championships has a long history of welcoming some of the top players in the game.
 
In our ninth year as proud sponsors, we hosted clients from around the world, giving them the opportunity to connect and enjoy this unparalleled event.
 
#ddftennis #jpmorganprivatebank

Are small- and mid-cap stocks the hidden gems of the stock market? Their attractive valuation and potential for next cycle leadership prove they might be. Tap the link in our bio to read #TopMarketTakeaways for more.

node.text:
One year ago this week: Russia invaded Ukraine. This saw European natural gas prices skyrocket a historic +300% in six months, catalyzing an acute energy and humanitarian crisis through the region. Since then, falling energy prices has alleviated the cost-of-living crisis and improved the European growth outlook. In fact, European equities have outpaced the U.S. so far this year and we expect this trend to continue in 2023.
 
For more on our latest views, be sure to check out our recent #TopMarketTakeaways.

node.text: At J.P. Morgan Private Bank, it’s our priority to create a diverse advisor population. Hear from our colleagues who share why they believe reflective representation in the Asian American community strengthens our workplace.
What are the latest observations investors should consider? Tap the link in our bio to read this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways for insights across the market and economy.

The #SundanceFilmFestival is the ultimate gathering of original storytellers and audiences who are seeking new voices and fresh perspectives in film—and we had the chance to catch up with two industry-leading experts.
 
Our own David Shaheen, Managing Director and Group Head of J.P. Morgan Entertainment Industries and the West Region, moderated a conversation with Peter Benedek, Founding Partner and Board member of United Talent Agency, and Erik Feig, Founder and CEO of PICTURESTART.
 
#jpmorganprivatebank

There are two types of assets we’re watching closely: those that could help investors weather an economic downturn, and those whose prices already reflect that outcome. We think semiconductor stocks fall squarely in the second camp and could be priced right for portfolios in 2023.
 
Tap the link in our bio for the full article.

We think philanthropy should be practiced year-round—and donors across the world seem to agree. Discover the giving trends that have been gaining worldwide momentum. Tap the link in our bio to read the article.

Groundbreaking premieres. Exciting features. Outstanding connections. We’re delighted to be a partner for this year’s Sundance Film Festival, and we’re ready to experience new voices and fresh perspectives from so many talented artists.
